model_narrative: We plan a network of warehouses that serve a number of demand regions. We decide which warehouses to open and, for each region, which open warehouse will serve it. Opening a warehouse incurs a fixed cost, and serving a region from a warehouse incurs a transportation cost. We also track the resulting total fixed cost, total transportation cost, and overall total cost. The objective is to minimize the overall total cost.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the set of candidate warehouses that may be opened

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the set of demand regions that must each be served

params.name: f
params.doc: the fixed cost incurred to open each warehouse, in cost units

params.name: c
params.doc: the capacity of each warehouse, expressed in the same units as region demand

params.name: d
params.doc: the demand of each region, expressed in the same units as warehouse capacity

params.name: t
params.doc: the transportation cost of serving a region from a warehouse, in cost units; indexed by region first and warehouse second

vars.name: totcost
vars.doc: the total cost of the chosen plan, in cost units

vars.name: fcost
vars.doc: the total fixed cost of opening warehouses, in cost units

vars.name: tcost
vars.doc: the total transportation cost of serving regions, in cost units

vars.name: ow
vars.doc: warehouse-open indicator; equals 1 if the warehouse is opened and 0 otherwise

vars.name: oa
vars.doc: shipment-arc indicator; equals 1 if the region is served from the warehouse and 0 otherwise

description: The total cost must equal the fixed cost of the warehouses opened plus the transportation cost of serving the regions.

description: The total fixed cost must equal the sum, over all warehouses, of each warehouse's fixed opening cost counted only when that warehouse is opened.
expected_pyomo: model.deffcost = Constraint(expr=model.fcost == sum(model.f[i] * model.ow[i] for i in model.i))

model_narrative: We plan a network of warehouses that serve a number of demand regions. We decide which warehouses to open and, for each region, which open warehouse will serve it. Opening a warehouse incurs a fixed cost, and serving a region from a warehouse incurs a transportation cost. We also track the resulting total fixed cost, total transportation cost, and overall total cost. The objective is to minimize the overall total cost.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the set of candidate warehouses that may be opened

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the set of demand regions that must each be served

params.name: f
params.doc: the fixed cost incurred to open each warehouse, in cost units

params.name: c
params.doc: the capacity of each warehouse, expressed in the same units as region demand

params.name: d
params.doc: the demand of each region, expressed in the same units as warehouse capacity

params.name: t
params.doc: the transportation cost of serving a region from a warehouse, in cost units; indexed by region first and warehouse second

vars.name: totcost
vars.doc: the total cost of the chosen plan, in cost units

vars.name: fcost
vars.doc: the total fixed cost of opening warehouses, in cost units

vars.name: tcost
vars.doc: the total transportation cost of serving regions, in cost units

vars.name: ow
vars.doc: warehouse-open indicator; equals 1 if the warehouse is opened and 0 otherwise

vars.name: oa
vars.doc: shipment-arc indicator; equals 1 if the region is served from the warehouse and 0 otherwise

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, the total cost must equal the fixed cost of the warehouses opened plus the transportation cost of serving the regions. Second, the total fixed cost must equal the sum over all warehouses of each warehouse's fixed opening cost counted only when that warehouse is opened. Third, the total transportation cost must equal the sum over every region and warehouse pairing of the cost of serving that region from that warehouse counted only when the region is actually served from it. Fourth, for each warehouse the combined demand of all regions it serves cannot exceed that warehouse's capacity. Fifth, each region must be served by exactly one warehouse. Finally, a region can be served from a warehouse only if that warehouse is open.
expected_pyomo:
model.deftotcost = Constraint(expr=model.totcost == model.fcost + model.tcost)
model.deffcost = Constraint(expr=model.fcost == sum(model.f[i] * model.ow[i] for i in model.i))
model.deftcost = Constraint(expr=model.tcost == sum(model.t[j, i] * model.oa[i, j] for i in model.i for j in model.j))
def defwcap_rule(m, i):
    return sum(m.d[j] * m.oa[i, j] for j in m.j) <= m.c[i]
model.defwcap = Constraint(model.i, rule=defwcap_rule)
def onew_rule(m, j):
    return sum(m.oa[i, j] for i in m.i) == 1
model.onew = Constraint(model.j, rule=onew_rule)
def defow_rule(m, i, j):
    return m.ow[i] >= m.oa[i, j]
model.defow = Constraint(model.i, model.j, rule=defow_rule)
